verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal palmitoylation and myristoylation. It is a cytoplasmic protein with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. While it associates with surface receptors as a signaling partner, this is transient non-covalent recruitment via adaptors—not stable surface anchoring. No documented cancer ecto-pool, cell-death translocation, tissue-restricted surface display, lysosomal exocytosis, or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. Inner-leaflet lipidation orients the protein toward the cytoplasm, not the extracellular space.
